turns.body_text: As a note - we will NOT be making changes to the transfer process when it comes to Central Warehouse.     Sites can not receive items that are not reflected as "Shipped" from the warehouse, and the warehouse must initiate their own Transfer.     There is a perpetual inventory for the warehouse that would be impacted if sites were able to add items that were not on their ship ticket... if all items in the warehouse are "committed" - then there are none available for the site to be able to "add to receipt."
turns.body_html:
As a note - we will NOT be making changes to the transfer process when it comes to Central Warehouse.

Sites can not receive items that are not reflected as "Shipped" from the warehouse, and the warehouse must initiate their own Transfer.

There is a perpetual inventory for the warehouse that would be impacted if sites were able to add items that were not on their ship ticket... if all items in the warehouse are "committed" - then there are none available for the site to be able to "add to receipt."

turns.body_text:
Good morning,     We will not be making changes to the functionality for transfers or receipts where the warehouse is involved in the transaction.            Sites can not receive items that are not reflected as "Shipped" from the warehouse, and the warehouse must initiate their own Transfer.     There is a perpetual inventory for the warehouse that would be impacted if sites were able to add items that were not on their ship ticket... if all items in the warehouse are "committed" - then there are none (or no extra quantities) available for the site to be able to "add to receipt."     We do allow for a site to receive LESS than the quantity listed on the ship ticket, as there is an allowance for items being damaged en route... and the appropriate adjustments for those scenarios can be performed from within the warehouse distribution.                 ✅ Complete your Year Begin Tasks ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation

turns.body_html:
Good morning,

We will not be making changes to the functionality for transfers or receipts where the warehouse is involved in the transaction.

Sites can not receive items that are not reflected as "Shipped" from the warehouse, and the warehouse must initiate their own Transfer.

There is a perpetual inventory for the warehouse that would be impacted if sites were able to add items that were not on their ship ticket... if all items in the warehouse are "committed" - then there are none (or no extra quantities) available for the site to be able to "add to receipt."

We do allow for a site to receive LESS than the quantity listed on the ship ticket, as there is an allowance for items being damaged en route... and the appropriate adjustments for those scenarios can be performed from within the warehouse distribution.

turns.author: "customercare@primeroedge.com" <customercare@primeroedge.com>
turns.body_text:
Hello Leah Anne,     It's not only about the "current" settings when working with the transfer process... if the item has EVER been available at the site being selected, then it can receive that item via a transfer.      Likewise, if the site were to select "All Items" when performing a physical inventory, every single item they've ever had access to will show up for counting.      In the transfers area, this helps when there are instances where districts need a site to have "once per year" access to something, and allowing it in the transfer process makes it so that they don't have to go change the configuration of the item to get it there, and then go change it back...  for the physical inventory, the "All Items" access allows sites to be able to enter counts for products that may no longer be orderable/receivable, but they might still have some on hand that needs to be counted with the physical inventory.      It happens most often for things that are used only at school events on very specific occasions. (Pep Rally's, Bake Sales, Grandparents Breakfast, etc.)        ✅ Complete your Year Begin Tasks ✨ Explore PrimeroEdge’s fresh look: reimagined.primeroedge.com ? See the Navigation

replying all to this ticket         Schrenia Bockholt | CYBERSOFT  Business Analyst, Product Management  PrimeroEdge | SchoolCafé
turns.body_html:
Hello Leah Anne,

It's not only about the "current" settings when working with the transfer process... if the item has EVER been available at the site being selected, then it can receive that item via a transfer. 

Likewise, if the site were to select "All Items" when performing a physical inventory, every single item they've ever had access to will show up for counting. 

In the transfers area, this helps when there are instances where districts need a site to have "once per year" access to something, and allowing it in the transfer process makes it so that they don't have to go change the configuration of the item to get it there, and then go change it back...  for the physical inventory, the "All Items" access allows sites to be able to enter counts for products that may no longer be orderable/receivable, but they might still have some on hand that needs to be counted with the physical inventory. 

It happens most often for things that are used only at school events on very specific occasions. (Pep Rally's, Bake Sales, Grandparents Breakfast, etc.)

turns.body_text: Maggie called us in regards to being unable to transfer the item Utility Cart/Bussing - (6062)     After some investigation, we were unable to find any sort of count for said item.   I was confirming with Maggie again that she did have the items in her inventory before creating a ticket, and when she went to double-check, she realized they hadn't been added. We both shared a laugh and made sure the items showed up for the Transfer Items after she updated her inventory.      All is good, and this ticket will be closed.
turns.body_html:
Maggie called us in regards to being unable to transfer the item Utility Cart/Bussing - (6062)

After some investigation, we were unable to find any sort of count for said item. 
I was confirming with Maggie again that she did have the items in her inventory before creating a ticket, and when she went to double-check, she realized they hadn't been added. We both shared a laugh and made sure the items showed up for the Transfer Items after she updated her inventory. 

All is good, and this ticket will be closed.

turns.author: agent:22052164409
turns.body_text: Inventory > Transfers/Withdrawals > Transfer Items   Entered in the From and To sites. The Item List will display below, giving you the option to select the Item Category / Item Description / Whole vs Broken Unit quantity.     Inventory > Reports > Items   General Search of items within the district's history.      Inventory > Transfers/Withdrawals > Transfer List   Looked up the "From Site" and located the "To Site".    Checked the comments to locate "Small Equipment".
turns.body_html:
Inventory > Transfers/Withdrawals > Transfer Items 
Entered in the From and To sites. The Item List will display below, giving you the option to select the Item Category / Item Description / Whole vs Broken Unit quantity.

Inventory > Reports > Items 
General Search of items within the district's history. 

Inventory > Transfers/Withdrawals > Transfer List 
Looked up the "From Site" and located the "To Site".  
Checked the comments to locate "Small Equipment".

turns.body_text: Hi Terri/ Keith,      Never mind. All the above details are Not Valid because the Inventory License has been removed for the Central Warehouse site for Some reason.     I am not sure why these changes were made.  Also, this has nothing to do with the Version DEPLOYMENT because the version was deployed on 01/19/2026, and these license changes happened on 01/15/2026 and 01/16/2026.      The Central Warehouse will need both Central Warehouse license and also the Inventory License in order for it to work as a Central Warehouse site in Inventory module.      Since the Site has only Central Warehouse license is why its not getting list of the above mentioned Pages.  Please see why it was removed and add it back as needed.
turns.body_html:
Hi Terri/ Keith, 

Never mind. All the above details are Not Valid because the Inventory License has been removed for the Central Warehouse site for Some reason.

I am not sure why these changes were made.  Also, this has nothing to do with the Version DEPLOYMENT because the version was deployed on 01/19/2026, and these license changes happened on 01/15/2026 and 01/16/2026. 

The Central Warehouse will need both Central Warehouse license and also the Inventory License in order for it to work as a Central Warehouse site in Inventory module. 

Since the Site has only Central Warehouse license is why its not getting list of the above mentioned Pages.  Please see why it was removed and add it back as needed.

turns.body_html:
Item 100313 corn

Customer is asking to transfer item from Aug 2025 at northeast elem to warehouse.

turns.author: Burnell Offord <bofford@slpsb.org>
turns.body_text:
CAUTION: This email originated from outside of the organization. Do not click links or open attachments unless you recognize the sender and know the content is safe.

Thank you for your assistance.  Let me further explain the problem.

I am trying to enter inventory for August 2025.  I entered the Quantity on Hand, as I was instructed, and then proceeded to do transfers to individual schools.
My initial quantity on hand for Corn (Item # 100313) prior to August 2025 was 8.  On August 1, 2025, I received 75 cases of corn.  Therefore, I entered the
Quantity on Hand for August 2025 to be 83.  83 currently shows as the Quantity on Hand.  I did not know that I should have gone to the Transfer/Withdrawals;
Add to Inventory tab and add the 75 cases of corn there.

Next I transferred items from the Warehouse to individual schools.  The date that I used was August 5, 2025.

When I tried to transfer corn to Northeast Elementary the system did not allow me to transfer the corn because it thought there was no corn left to transfer.
I had previously transferred a total of 8 corn to the below schools:
   Grand Coteau Elem = 1 case
   Leonville Elem = 2 cases
   Beau Chene High - 1 case
   Port Barre Elem = 1 case
   Magnet Academy for Cultural Arts = 1 case
   Opelousas Jr. High School = 1 case
   CAPS = 1 case
The above totals 8 cases of corn.  8 was my initial count so according to the system, I have no more corn in the warehouse to transfer.
Although I entered the Quantity on Hand to be 83, the system is not acknowledging that count because I did not know that I was
supposed to go to the Add to Inventory tab and enter the 75 cans of corn that I received on August 1, 2025.

Today I spoke with a Primero Edge Representative and while on the phone with her, I attempted to add the 75 cases of corn to the Warehouse,
but I did not realize that the system had defaulted to Arnaudville so I wrongly added the 75 cases of corn to Arnaudville.  Then I tried to transfer
the corn from Arnaudville to the warehouse but I noticed it had today's date instead of August 1, 2025.  At this time, I need the 75 cases of corn
to be added to the warehouse inventory.

I was also unable to transfer 1 case of French Fries from the warehouse to Opelousas Senior High School as of 8/5/2025.

I think that in addition to entering the Quantity on Hand, I was supposed to "Add to Inventory" all items that I had received for 8/1/2025.  Those items
which I received on 8/1/2025 and need to add to the "Add to Inventory" tab are:

[image.png]

The above items were received 8/1/2025 and so I need to add them to the Inventory (Add to Inventory tab).

I already included them in the Quantity on Hand.

After reading the above explanation, I would like for someone to give me a call, please.
Thank you.


Ms. Burnell Offord
Nutrition Coordinator
St. Landry Parish School Board
Phone:  (337) 948-3657  Ext. 10737
